返回本批次

litellm 1.82.8 PyPI 包中的恶意 litellm_init.pth – 凭证窃取程序

Malicious litellm_init.pth in litellm 1.82.8 PyPI package – credential stealer

github.com 作者 theanonymousone 分数 724 评论 1 2026/03/24 20:36
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. [LITELLM 团队] - 有关团队的更新，请参阅：#24518
  2. 摘要 PyPI 上的 litellm==1.82.8 轮包包含一个恶意 .pth 文件（litellm_init.pth，34,628 字节），每次 Python 解释器启动时，该文件都会自动执行凭据窃取脚本 - 无需导入 litellm。这是供应链的妥协。恶意文件列在程序包自己的记录中： litellm_init.pth,sha256=ceNa7wMJnNHy1kRnNCcwJaFjWX3pORLfMh7xGL8TUjg,34628
  3. 复制 pip download litellm==1.82.8 --no-deps -d /tmp/check python3 -c " import zipfile, os whl = '/tmp/check/' + [f for f in os.listdir('/tmp/check') if f.endswith('.whl')][0] with zipfile.ZipFile(whl) as z: pth = [n for n in z.namelist() if n.endswith('.pth')] print('PTH files:', pth) for p in pth: print(z.read(p)[:300]) " 您将看到 litellm_init.pth 包含： import os, subprocess, sys; subprocess.Popen([sys.executable, "-c", "import base64; exec(base64.b64decode('...'))"]) 恶意行为（全面分析） 有效负载采用双 Base64 编码。解码后，它执行以下操作： 第 1 阶段：信息收集 该脚本从主机系统收集敏感数据：
  4. 系统信息：主机名、whoami、uname -a、ip 地址、ip 路由 环境变量：printenv（捕获所有 API 密钥、秘密、令牌） SSH 密钥：~/.ssh/id_rsa、~/.ssh/id_ed25519、~/.ssh/id_ecdsa、~/.ssh/id_dsa、~/.ssh/authorized_keys、 ~/.ssh/known_hosts、~/.ssh/config Git 凭证：~/.gitconfig、~/.git-credentials AWS 凭证：~/.aws/credentials、~/.aws/config、IMDS 令牌 + 安全凭证 Kubernetes 机密：~/.kube/config、/etc/kubernetes/admin.conf、/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf、 /etc/kubernetes/controller-manager.conf、/etc/kubernetes/scheduler.conf、服务帐户令牌 GCP 凭据：~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json Azure 凭据：~/.azure/ Docker 配置：~/.docker/config.json、/kaniko/.docker/config.json、/root/.docker/config.json 包管理器配置：~/.npmrc、 ~/.vault-token、~/.netrc、~/.lftprc、~/.msmtprc、~/.my.cnf、~/.pgpass、~/.mongorc.js Shell 历史记录：~/.bash_history、~/.zsh_history、~/.sh_history、~/.mysql_history、~/.psql_history、~/.rediscli_history 加密钱包： ~/.bitcoin/、~/.litecoin/、~/.dogecoin/、~/.zcash/、~/.dashcore/、~/.ripple/、~/.bitmonero/、~/.ethereum/keystore/、~/.cardano/、~/.config/solana/ SSL/TLS 私钥：/etc/ssl/private/、让我们加密 .pem 和 .key 文件 CI/CD 机密： terraform.tfvars、.gitlab-ci.yml、.travis.yml、Jenkinsfile、.drone.yml、Anchor.toml、ansible.cfg 数据库凭据：PostgreSQL、MySQL、Redis、LDAP 配置文件 Webhook URL：grep 用于 env 和配置文件中的 Slack/Discord webhook URL
  5. 第二阶段：加密和渗透

Original Summary

  1. [LITELLM TEAM] - For updates from the team, please see: #24518
  2. Summary The litellm==1.82.8 wheel package on PyPI contains a malicious .pth file (litellm_init.pth, 34,628 bytes) that automatically executes a credential-stealing script every time the Python interpreter starts — no import litellm required. This is a supply chain compromise. The malicious file is listed in the package's own RECORD: litellm_init.pth,sha256=ceNa7wMJnNHy1kRnNCcwJaFjWX3pORLfMh7xGL8TUjg,34628
  3. Reproduction pip download litellm==1.82.8 --no-deps -d /tmp/check python3 -c " import zipfile, os whl = '/tmp/check/' + [f for f in os.listdir('/tmp/check') if f.endswith('.whl')][0] with zipfile.ZipFile(whl) as z: pth = [n for n in z.namelist() if n.endswith('.pth')] print('PTH files:', pth) for p in pth: print(z.read(p)[:300]) " You will see litellm_init.pth containing: import os, subprocess, sys; subprocess.Popen([sys.executable, "-c", "import base64; exec(base64.b64decode('...'))"]) Malicious Behavior (full analysis) The payload is double base64-encoded. When decoded, it performs the following: Stage 1: Information Collection The script collects sensitive data from the host system:
  4. System info: hostname, whoami, uname -a, ip addr, ip route Environment variables: printenv (captures all API keys, secrets, tokens) SSH keys: ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.ssh/id_ecdsa, ~/.ssh/id_dsa, ~/.ssh/authorized_keys, ~/.ssh/known_hosts, ~/.ssh/config Git credentials: ~/.gitconfig, ~/.git-credentials AWS credentials: ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, IMDS token + security credentials Kubernetes secrets: ~/.kube/config, /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf, /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf, /etc/kubernetes/controller-manager.conf, /etc/kubernetes/scheduler.conf, service account tokens GCP credentials: ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json Azure credentials: ~/.azure/ Docker configs: ~/.docker/config.json, /kaniko/.docker/config.json, /root/.docker/config.json Package manager configs: ~/.npmrc, ~/.vault-token, ~/.netrc, ~/.lftprc, ~/.msmtprc, ~/.my.cnf, ~/.pgpass, ~/.mongorc.js Shell history: ~/.bash_history, ~/.zsh_history, ~/.sh_history, ~/.mysql_history, ~/.psql_history, ~/.rediscli_history Crypto wallets: ~/.bitcoin/, ~/.litecoin/, ~/.dogecoin/, ~/.zcash/, ~/.dashcore/, ~/.ripple/, ~/.bitmonero/, ~/.ethereum/keystore/, ~/.cardano/, ~/.config/solana/ SSL/TLS private keys: /etc/ssl/private/, Let's Encrypt .pem and .key files CI/CD secrets: terraform.tfvars, .gitlab-ci.yml, .travis.yml, Jenkinsfile, .drone.yml, Anchor.toml, ansible.cfg Database credentials: PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis, LDAP config files Webhook URLs: grep for Slack/Discord webhook URLs in env and config files
  5. Stage 2: Encryption & Exfiltration
